affected_pattern: Customer usage 10x normal correlates with a video streaming app update date
root_cause: Streaming app default changed to ultra-HD — a popular streaming app pushed an update that defaulted to 4K streaming on cellular, multiplying data usage for all its users on the mobile network.
supporting_evidence: Usage Spike RCA Playbook: Streaming-App Default Bitrate Change pattern
User-agent on high-usage sessions matches the app update version
Public release notes confirm the default-4K change
recommended_action: Customer-facing advisory with instructions to change app settings
Offer one-time goodwill credit and a data-booster option
Engage the streaming partner about lowering cellular default bitrate
Add traffic-classification telemetry so future partner-driven shifts are detected faster
